evidence_text: The workflow used a main dialogue as an orchestrator agent that explicitly delegated individual tasks to sub-agents, then verified and integrated their results.
source_excerpt: "Approach: the main dialogue (or session in terms of OpenCOde) is the an orchestrator agent; you explicitly ask it to delegate individual tasks to sub-agents ... verify them, and integrate the results."
note: This is a direct description of the workflow the author says was used.
caveat: This is a direct description of the workflow the author says was used.

evidence_text: The stated reason for delegating to sub-agents was to avoid hitting the model context window limit.
source_excerpt: "Why? Cause we don't want to hit the context window limit of the model."
note: This source states the intended rationale for the delegation pattern.
caveat: This source states the intended rationale for the delegation pattern.

evidence_text: The task in the experiment was to rewrite a previously vibe-coded LiteLLM provider that cascades requests across several LLMs before returning a final response.
source_excerpt: "Task: rewrite a previously vibe-coded provider for litellm which implements a cascade of requests to several LLMs ... before returning a final response."
note: The source identifies the implementation target and intended behavior.
caveat: The source identifies the implementation target and intended behavior.

evidence_text: The reported agent work time was about 4 hours.
source_excerpt:
"Results:
About 4 hours of pure agent work time"
note: This is a self-reported duration from the author.
caveat: This is a self-reported duration from the author.

evidence_text: The orchestrator session was reported as costing $4.13 and reaching 157k tokens of dialogue length by the end of the task.
source_excerpt: "Orchestrator session — $4.13, 157k tokens of dialogue length by the end of the task"
note: These figures are reported by the author for the main session only.
caveat: These figures are reported by the author for the main session only.

evidence_text: The experiment used 16 sub-agent sessions with a reported total cost of $9.73.
note: This metric is reported separately from the orchestrator session figures.
caveat: This metric is reported separately from the orchestrator session figures.

evidence_text: The reported total spend was $13.86 for about 2 million tokens.
note: This is the aggregate cost and token estimate stated by the author.
caveat: This is the aggregate cost and token estimate stated by the author.

evidence_text: The work reportedly changed 26 files in Git.
note: The source gives a file-change count but no diff-level detail.
caveat: The source gives a file-change count but no diff-level detail.

evidence_text: The author reports that only 5 tests were written and that all of them were green.
note: The source presents passing tests, but the stated test count is small, which bounds what this outcome alone can show about coverage or reliability.
caveat: The source presents passing tests, but the stated test count is small, which bounds what this outcome alone can show about coverage or reliability.

evidence_text: The author claims the app works, with the provider executing multiple LLM queries and the Streamlit dashboard showing recorded traces.
source_excerpt: "The app works — the provider executes multiple llm queries aggregating the final respond, the Streamlit dashboard shows the recorded traces."
note: This is a self-reported functional outcome; the same source also reports limited test count, so the operational claim should remain attached to that limitation.
caveat: This is a self-reported functional outcome; the same source also reports limited test count, so the operational claim should remain attached to that limitation.

evidence_text: For about 4 hours of agent time, the author reports about half an hour of human effort and about 10 user messages.
source_excerpt: "For these ~4 hours of agent time, it took about half an hour of human effort and ~10 user messages."
note: This is a self-reported measure of human involvement.
caveat: This is a self-reported measure of human involvement.

evidence_text: The author states that the orchestrator/sub-agent approach fit about 2 million tokens of work into a 157K-token main thread and says GPT-5.2 Codex still had room within a 400K context window.
source_excerpt: "Approach: the main dialogue (or session in terms of OpenCOde) is the an orchestrator agent; you explicitly ask it to delegate individual tasks to sub-agents ... verify them, and integrate the results."
note: This is a direct description of the workflow the author says was used.
caveat: This is a direct description of the workflow the author says was used.
